title: Technical Support Specialist
description: We are seeking a proactive Desktop Support Analyst, with experience in customer\-facing environments, to join our IT support team. This role is critical in ensuring end users receive a high standard of technical assistance across hardware, software, and enterprise applications. You will play a key role in maintaining business productivity by resolving incidents efficiently and delivering an exceptional user experience. Key Responsibilities * Diagnose and resolve issues with desktops, laptops, and otther office/IT devices. * Troubleshoot issues operating systems (Windows 11\), Microsoft 365 applications, and other software issues and errors. * Manage incidents and requests through a ticketing platform. * Manage accounts across Active Directory, email platforms, etc. * Install, configure, and update software. * Support users by addressing and resolving issues using remote management tools. * Prepare new equipment including imaging, asset tagging, and software deployment. * Ensure all devices are maintained to a high standard and that all antivirus, encryption, and software is up to date. What you'll bring * Minimum 2 years' IT and technical supoport experience, ideally with Microsoft 365 services including Teams and OneDrive. * Hands\-on experience with ITSM ticketing systems \- essential. * Knowledge of mobile device support and Mobile Device Management (MDM) solutions. * Strong background in desktop hardware troubleshooting and end\-user support. Desirable skills \& capabilities * Very good communication skills. * Ability to maintain documentation to a good and comprehensive standard. * A "customer first" approach. * Strong time and workload management skill. * Strong problem\-solving and analytical skills. If you are a driven Desktop Support professional looking for an opportunity to make a tangible impact within a dynamic organisation, we would love to hear from you. Apply today.

title: Technical Writer
description: **Team \& Role Overview** The **Growth Platform (EHQ) team** powers large\-scale, experiment\-driven experiences across. EHQ tooling is used by engineering and non\-engineering partners (marketing, content specialists, data analysts) to configure, launch, and measure complex experiences. Today, these teams rely on a mix of wikis, internal notes, and UI copy that can be inconsistent or incomplete, making configuration time\-consuming and increasing support overhead for engineers. We are seeking a **Technical Writer** to own and elevate documentation for EHQ. You’ll work closely with engineering, product, design, and product marketing to define documentation standards, close important gaps, and embed documentation into the feature release process as a first‑class deliverable. This is a **5 months (3 days per week, 60 working days) fixed duration role** focused on achieving a strong, maintainable state for EHQ documentation, after which ongoing changes will follow the best\-practices and standards that you created. **What You’ll Do** * **Audit and improve existing documentation** * Review existing wikis, EHQ UI, and internal notes for accuracy and completeness * Partner with engineers to correct inaccuracies and rewrite content in plain language for non\-engineers * **Collaborate during development** * Work with engineers, PM, PMM and design to expand documentation as features are built * Ensure every new capability ships with clear self\-serve setup instructions, examples, and known nuances * **Establish documentation standards and workflows** * Create and maintain a **framework** for documentation standards (structure, terminology, examples, logging definitions) * Make documentation expectations explicit in feature requirements, while still expecting engineers to contribute content as part of their responsibilities * **Focus on self\-serve and role\-based journeys** * Produce role\-/recipe\-based flows that match how frequent customers (e.g., lifecycle, CRM, campaign specialists) actually use EHQ This role is expected to both **improve existing documentation** and **create net\-new coverage** for gaps identified in the appendix below. **Metrics for Success** We will evaluate impact through: * **Reduced support tickets and escalations** related to EHQ configuration and behavior * **Positive feedback from users and stakeholders** (e.g., PMM, campaign specialists, lifecycle and CRM teams) on clarity and ease of setup * **Documentation coverage and freshness** * % of key EHQ surfaces with complete documentation * Content that is clearly timestamped and kept in sync with product changes We also expect **time to configure and launch EHQ experiences** to improve in combination with UI changes and better comprehension, even if we lack a perfect historical benchmark today. **Basic Qualifications** * Experience writing for technical platforms used by non\-engineers * Ability to collaborate closely with engineers, PM, design, and PMM * Strength in structuring role\-based and recipe\-based documentation and guided flows * Demonstrated ability to use AI to improve speed and quality in your day\-to\-day workflow for relevant outputs. **Education** * 4\+ years experience in a technical writer role. * Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or related technical field, or equivalent practical experience.

title: Trainee Commissioning Engineer (Full Time)
description: **JOB DESCRIPTION: TRAINEE HVAC COMMISSIONING ENGINEER** **REPORTS TO: OPERATIONS MANAGER** **Position: Full time (IMMEDIATE START – May 2026\)** **Salary: €32,000 \- €40,000 \+ company vehicle, overtime, annual bonus and pension** **Email application: info@sibsgroup.com** **Requirements.** Interested applicants should possess a diploma or degree in building service or mechanical engineering. A previous background in site\-based commissioning practices would be advantageous but is not essential as in house training will be provided. The role would require a person with excellent diagnostic and problem\-solving abilities coupled with the ability to work to tight deadlines. Excellent oral and written communication skills to communicate effectively through all level of the organization along with a high level of proficiency with Microsoft Office programs would also be a requirement. **Primary roles \& responsibilities** · Commissioning of ventilation and Hydronic (LPHW \& CHW) systems. · Maintain a smooth commissioning process of a project by coordinating between the company and the client. · Review commissioning plans in accordance with project specifications. · Review design data and expedite onsite commissioning activities and report to both internal and external stakeholders. · Prepare and maintain commission records of site observations, testing processes and checklists. · Track and review progress of projects. · Compile commission report data for summarising. · Assist in summarising and updating commission records. · Complete commissioning/handover report upon project completion. · Ensure weekly time sheets \& expenses are submitted in a timely manner. · Maintain an empowering and inspiring work culture with the commissioning team. · Attain customer satisfaction through adhering to client guidelines with the entire project team. · Execute all projects in accordance with H\&S requirements of SIBS and the client. **About Us:** SIBS Group was established in Ireland in 1991 as a commissioning services service provider, the company has evolved over the last 30 years to become a specialist solution provider in HVAC \& Water Systems Commissioning and Validation internationally. As a specialist TAB (Testing and Balancing) company, SiBS commission and validate chilled water, heating, ventilation and air conditioning systems for MEP contractors and building owners. Over our 30 years in business, we have gained significant experience in the pharmaceutical, healthcare, semi\-conductor, chemical, food processing, manufacturing, education, administration centres and hotel sectors and have grown to where we have ongoing projects over two continents. We offer competitive salaries and the ability to progress your career with an industry leading company. Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent, Graduate Pay: €32,000\.00\-€40,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Company car * Company pension * Food allowance * Sick pay Education: * Advanced/Higher Certificate (required) Licence/Certification: * Driving Licence (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Urban Design Graduate
description: **Urban Design Graduate** **Why Join This Employer?** Join a leading architectural studio dedicated to purposeful design, where you’ll help create environments that improve how people live, learn, work, and connect. Collaborate with an experienced multidisciplinary team that combines Irish expertise with international best practices to deliver innovative and well\-crafted design solutions. Work on a broad range of projects in residential, education, workplace, healthcare with a focus on building sustainable communities and enhancing people's day\-to\-day experiences. **Your Key Responsibilities** * As an Urban Design Graduate, you will support the design and delivery of meaningful urban projects, collaborating closely with senior professionals and cross\-functional teams. * Take part in creating project layouts, masterplans, and public spaces that balance community needs, context, and planning frameworks. * Use Revit to prepare high\-quality models, drawings, and visualisations to communicate design concepts clearly at all stages. * Assist with design presentations, planning applications, and project documentation, supporting the team to achieve strong outcomes. * Gain hands\-on exposure by engaging with clients, local authorities, and stakeholders—growing your experience in all aspects of urban design delivery. **What You Need to Succeed** * 2 to 3 years’ experience in urban design, architecture, or a related field. * Proficiency in Revit, with the ability to produce detailed models and visualisations independently. * Sound understanding of core urban design principles and Irish planning policy/guidelines. * Confident communicator both visually (drawings, presentations) and verbally; comfortable collaborating in a team. * Experience producing planning documentation, public realm layouts, and urban project drawings. * Strong initiative, eagerness to learn, effective organisational skills, and great attention to detail. **Rewards and Benefits** * Competitive salary based on your experience (€35,000–€42,000\). * Comprehensive benefits package, including health insurance. * Access to ongoing training, structured mentorship, and clear progression opportunities within an acclaimed architectural practice. **Next Steps** Click “Apply Now” or reach out directly on 01 474 4690\. If you’re an aspiring Urban Design Graduate seeking a supportive environment and meaningful project experience, we want to hear from you!
